title: Passive skill upgrade — LLM triage + parallel digest execution
current_state: All 13 passive skills produced raw metric counts ('67 TODOs', '178 swallowed exceptions'). No prioritisation, no separation of actionable findings from noise. Daily digest ran skills sequentially (~30s wall clock). Together this made the daily report easy to ignore.
target_state: (a) agent/skill_triage.py exports triage() and deep_analysis(). Both use Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile primary, Claude Haiku fallback. Standalone-safe (auto-loads .env). (b) 4 metric skills wired with triage: tech_debt_accumulator, half_baked_feature_detector, ticket_candidate_miner, silent_failure_watcher. Each produces a 'Top concerns / Dismissed / Recommended action' section. (c) solution_lesson_distiller gets deep Haiku analysis to extract cross-cutting patterns from last 20 solutions. (d) passive_daily_digest.py uses ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4); scorecard order preserved; one skill failure does not abort the others.
fix_summary: NEW agent/skill_triage.py — triage() (Groq+Haiku fallback) and deep_analysis() (Haiku only) with lazy .env loader
scripts/passive/passive_daily_digest.py — parallel=True, max_workers=4; preserves SKILL_MODULES order in scorecard
NEW testing/test_skill_triage.py — 8 tests (providers, fallback chain, line truncation)
NEW testing/test_digest_parallel.py — 4 tests (parallelism, order preservation, exception isolation)
